title: Stelis rhodotantha
taxonomy: Stelis rhodotantha belongs to the family Orchidaceae, commonly known as the orchid family. It is a genus of orchids in the tribe Epidendreae. The tribe Epidendreae is further divided into several subtribes, and Stelis rhodotantha falls under the subtribe Pleurothallidinae. This classification suggests that Stelis rhodotantha shares characteristics with other orchids in the same subtribe. As a member of the Orchidaceae family, Stelis rhodotantha is closely related to other orchids, such as those in the genera Pleurothallis and Dracula.
uses: Stelis rhodotantha, a species of orchid, has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties in various parts of the world. Its root and stem extracts are reported to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. In traditional medicine, Stelis rhodotantha is used to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Additionally, its extracts are believed to possess antioxidant properties, which can help protect against cell damage. However, further research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Stelis rhodotantha for medicinal purposes.

intro: Stelis rhodotantha, also known as the Red-berried Stelis, is a species of orchid native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to soothe digestive issues and protect against cell damage.
